metadata.title: Guidance for Industry: Food-Effect Bioavailability and Fed Bioequivalence Studies

rules.rule_text: A food-effect BA study must be conducted for all new chemical entities (NCEs) during the IND period.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that a food-effect BA study be conducted for all new chemical entities (NCEs) during the IND period.

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA studies should be conducted early in the drug development process to guide and select formulations for further development and to provide information for Clinical Pharmacology and Dosage and Administration labeling sections.
rules.verbatim_quote: Food-effect BA studies should be conducted early in the drug development process to guide and select formulations for further development. Food-effect BA information should be available to design clinical safety and efficacy studies and to provide information for the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY and/or DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION sections of product labels.

rules.rule_text: If a sponsor makes changes in components, composition, and/or method of manufacture in the clinical trial formulation prior to approval, BE must be demonstrated between the to-be-marketed formulation and the clinical trial formulation.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a sponsor makes changes in components, composition, and/or method of manufacture in the clinical trial formulation prior to approval, BE should be demonstrated between the to-be-marketed formulation and the clinical trial formulation.

rules.rule_text: For ANDAs, a BE study under fed conditions is required for all orally administered immediate-release drug products, with limited exceptions.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to a BE study under fasting conditions, we recommend a BE study under fed conditions for all orally administered immediate-release drug products, with the following exceptions:
rules.exceptions: When both test product and RLD are rapidly dissolving, have similar dissolution profiles, and contain a BCS Class I drug substance; or when the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section of the RLD label states the product should be taken only on an empty stomach; or when the RLD label does not make any statements about food effect on absorption or administration.

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA and fed BE studies must be performed for all modified-release dosage forms.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that food-effect BA and fed BE studies be performed for all modified-release dosage forms.

rules.rule_text: For modified-release ANDAs, a BE study under fed conditions must be conducted in addition to a BE study under fasting conditions.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to a BE study under fasting conditions, a BE study under fed conditions should be conducted for all orally administered modified-release drug products.

rules.rule_text: For postapproval changes in an approved immediate- or modified-release drug product that requires in vivo redocumentation of BE under fasting conditions, fed BE studies are generally unnecessary.
rules.source_section: Section III (Recommendations for Food-Effect BA and Fed BE Studies)
rules.verbatim_quote: For postapproval changes in an approved immediate- or modified-release drug product that requires in vivo redocumentation of BE under fasting conditions, fed BE studies are generally unnecessary.

rules.rule_text: The recommended study design is a randomized, balanced, single-dose, two-treatment (fed vs. fasting), two-period, two-sequence crossover design.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend a randomized, balanced, single-dose, two-treatment (fed vs. fasting), two-period, two-sequence crossover design for studying the effects of food on the BA of either an immediate-release or a modified-release drug product.
rules.exceptions: Sponsor may propose alternative study designs with scientific rationale and justification in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: For a fed BE study, both test and reference formulations are administered following a test meal (fed condition), using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence crossover design.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend a similar, two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence crossover design for a fed BE study except that the treatments should consist of both test and reference formulations administered following a test meal (fed condition).

rules.rule_text: An adequate washout period must separate the two treatments in food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: An adequate washout period should separate the two treatments in food-effect BA and fed BE studies.

rules.rule_text: Studies may be carried out in healthy volunteers; patient population is appropriate if safety concerns preclude enrollment of healthy subjects.
rules.verbatim_quote: Both food-effect BA and fed BE studies can be carried out in healthy volunteers drawn from the general population. Studies in the patient population are also appropriate if safety concerns preclude the enrollment of healthy subjects.
rules.exceptions: Patient population when safety concerns preclude healthy subjects.

rules.rule_text: A minimum of 12 subjects must complete the food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: A minimum of 12 subjects should complete the food-effect BA and fed BE studies.

rules.rule_text: In general, the highest strength of a drug product intended to be marketed should be tested in food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, the highest strength of a drug product intended to be marketed should be tested in food-effect BA and fed BE studies. In some cases, clinical safety concerns can prevent the use of the highest strength and warrant the use of lower strengths of the dosage form.
rules.exceptions: Clinical safety concerns may warrant use of lower strengths.

rules.rule_text: For ANDAs, the same lot and strength used in the fasting BE study must be tested in the fed BE study.
rules.verbatim_quote: For ANDAs, the same lot and strength used in the fasting BE study should be tested in the fed BE study.

rules.rule_text: The test meal for food-effect BA and fed BE studies must be a high-fat (approximately 50% of total caloric content), high-calorie (approximately 800 to 1000 calories) meal.
rules.verbatim_quote: A high-fat (approximately 50 percent of total caloric content of the meal) and high-calorie (approximately 800 to 1000 calories) meal is recommended as a test meal for food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.exceptions: In NDAs, sponsor may choose to conduct food-effect BA studies using meals with different combinations of fats, carbohydrates, and proteins for exploratory or label purposes, but one of the meals must be the high-fat, high-calorie test meal described.

rules.rule_text: The test meal must derive approximately 150, 250, and 500-600 calories from protein, carbohydrate, and fat, respectively.
rules.verbatim_quote: This test meal should derive approximately 150, 250, and 500-600 calories from protein, carbohydrate, and fat, respectively.
rules.exceptions: If the caloric breakdown is significantly different, the sponsor must provide a scientific rationale.

rules.rule_text: The caloric breakdown of the test meal must be provided in the study report.
rules.verbatim_quote: The caloric breakdown of the test meal should be provided in the study report. If the caloric breakdown of the meal is significantly different from the one described above, the sponsor should provide a scientific rationale for this difference.

rules.rule_text: For fasted treatments, subjects must fast overnight for at least 10 hours and be administered the drug product with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water.
rules.verbatim_quote: Fasted Treatments: Following an overnight fast of at least 10 hours, subjects should be administered the drug product with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water.

rules.rule_text: For fasted treatments, no food must be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose, and water must not be allowed for one hour before and after drug administration.
rules.verbatim_quote: No food should be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose. Water can be allowed as desired except for one hour before and after drug administration.

rules.rule_text: For fed treatments, subjects must fast overnight for at least 10 hours, start the recommended meal 30 minutes prior to drug administration, eat the meal in 30 minutes or less, and the drug product must be administered 30 minutes after start of the meal.
rules.verbatim_quote: Fed Treatments: Following an overnight fast of at least 10 hours, subjects should start the recommended meal 30 minutes prior to administration of the drug product. Study subjects should eat this meal in 30 minutes or less; however, the drug product should be administered 30 minutes after start of the meal.

rules.rule_text: For fed treatments, the drug product must be administered with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water, and no food must be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose.
rules.verbatim_quote: The drug product should be administered with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water. No food should be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose.

rules.rule_text: Subjects must receive standardized meals scheduled at the same time in each period of the study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Subjects should receive standardized meals scheduled at the same time in each period of the study.

rules.rule_text: Timed samples in biological fluid (usually plasma) must be collected to permit characterization of the complete shape of the plasma concentration-time profile for the parent drug.
rules.verbatim_quote: For both fasted and fed treatment periods, timed samples in biological fluid, usually plasma, should be collected from the subjects to permit characterization of the complete shape of the plasma concentration-time profile for the parent drug.
rules.exceptions: It may be advisable to measure other moieties in the plasma, such as active metabolites.

rules.rule_text: The following pharmacokinetic parameters must be obtained: AUC(0-inf), AUC(0-t), Cmax, Tmax, lag-time (tlag) for modified-release products if present, terminal elimination half-life, and other relevant pharmacokinetic parameters.
rules.verbatim_quote: The following exposure measures and pharmacokinetic parameters should be obtained from the resulting concentration-time curves for the test and reference products in food-effect BA and fed BE studies: Total exposure, or area under the concentration-time curve (AUC0-inf, AUC0-t); Peak exposure (Cmax); Time to peak exposure (Tmax); Lag-time (tlag) for modified-release products, if present; Terminal elimination half-life; Other relevant pharmacokinetic parameters

rules.rule_text: Log-transformation of exposure measurements (AUC and Cmax) prior to analysis is required, and the 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means must be provided for AUC(0-inf), AUC(0-t), and Cmax.
rules.verbatim_quote: Log-transformation of exposure measurements (AUC and Cmax) prior to analysis is recommended. The 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between test and reference products should be provided for AUC0-inf, AUC0-t, and Cmax.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: For NDA food-effect BA studies, the fasted treatment serves as the reference; for ANDA fed BE studies, the RLD administered under fed conditions serves as the reference treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: For IND or NDA food-effect BA studies, the fasted treatment serves as the reference. For ANDA fed BE studies, the RLD administered under fed condition serves as the reference treatment.

rules.rule_text: For an NDA, absence of food effect on BA is not established if the 90% CI for the ratio of geometric means between fed and fasted treatments is not contained in the equivalence limits of 80-125 percent for either AUC(0-inf) (or AUC(0-t) when appropriate) or Cmax.
rules.verbatim_quote: For an NDA, an absence of food effect on BA is not established if the 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between fed and fasted treatments, based on log-transformed data, is not contained in the equivalence limits of 80-125 percent for either AUC0-inf (AUC0-t when appropriate) or Cmax.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: For ANDA fed BE studies, BE is concluded when the 90% CI for the ratio of geometric means between test and RLD product is contained in the BE limits of 80-125 percent for AUC and Cmax.
rules.verbatim_quote: For an ANDA, BE of a test product to the RLD product under fed conditions is concluded when the 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between the test and RLD product, based on log-transformed data, is contained in the BE limits of 80-125 percent for AUC and Cmax.
rules.exceptions: No criterion applies to Tmax, but Tmax values for test and reference products are expected to be comparable based on clinical relevance.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: The effect of food on the absorption and BA of a drug product must be described in the Clinical Pharmacology section of labeling; the Dosage and Administration section must provide instructions for drug administration in relation to food based on clinical relevance.
rules.verbatim_quote: The effect of food on the absorption and BA of a drug product should be described in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section of the labeling. In addition, the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section of the labeling should provide instructions for drug administration in relation to food based on clinical relevance.

rules.rule_text: For NDA sprinkle formulations, additional in vivo relative BA studies must be performed by sprinkling the product on soft foods and comparing it to the product administered in the intact form on an empty stomach.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the labeling to indicate that the drug product can be sprinkled on soft foods, additional in vivo relative BA studies should be performed by sprinkling the product on the soft foods to be listed in the labeling (test treatment) and comparing it to the product administered in the intact form (reference treatment), then administering both on an empty stomach.
